rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical epoxidation of alkenes using water as the oxygen source, catalyzed by a sterically hindered manganese porphyrin (TMP)MnCl under non-halide-mediated conditions
electrochemical_role: Anodic single-electron oxidation of (TMP)Mn(III)-OH to (TMP)Mn(IV)=O, which either undergoes a second anodic SET or disproportionates to generate the active [(TMP)Mn(V)=O]+ oxidant; protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode

key_intermediates.name: (TMP)Mn(III)(OH) resting/active catalyst formed with pyridine and H2O
epoxide product via O-atom transfer to alkene

electron_flow: At the anode, (TMP)Mn(III)-OH is oxidized by SET to (TMP)Mn(IV)=O; a second SET (or disproportionation of two Mn(IV)=O units) generates [(TMP)Mn(V)=O]+, which transfers its oxygen atom to the alkene to give the epoxide and regenerate (TMP)Mn(III)-OH, while H2O supplies the oxygen and protons are reduced to H2 at the Pt cathode.
rate_determining_step: Generation of [(TMP)Mn(V)=O]+ via the second single-electron anodic oxidation / disproportionation of (TMP)Mn(IV)=O

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O isotope labeling
probe_reagents.observation: 18O incorporated into styrene oxide (2a'), confirming water as the oxygen source rather than O2

probe_reagents.name: N2 atmosphere control
probe_reagents.observation: Reaction proceeds under N2, ruling out molecular oxygen as the O source

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of (TMP)MnCl
probe_reagents.observation: Two redox peaks at 0.67 and 0.9 V vs Fc/Fc+ assigned to Mn(III)/Mn(IV) and Mn(IV)/Mn(V); reversible Mn(III)/Mn(IV) peak indicates stable tetravalent intermediate

probe_reagents.name: Constant potential electrolysis at 0.75 V vs Fc/Fc+
probe_reagents.observation: Styrene epoxidation gave 2a in 87% GC yield with 87% Faradaic efficiency despite potential being insufficient to oxidize Mn(IV)=O to Mn(V)=O directly, supporting disproportionation pathway

notes: Two parallel pathways are proposed for forming [(TMP)Mn(V)=O]+: (a) two consecutive anodic SETs on (TMP)Mn(III)(OH), and (b) anodic oxidation of (TMP)Mn(III)(OH) to (TMP)Mn(IV)=O followed by disproportionation into (TMP)Mn(III)(OH) + [(TMP)Mn(V)=O]+; the constant-potential experiment at 0.75 V favors the disproportionation route. The use of bulky TMP ligand is critical to enable SET-based generation of Mn(V)=O rather than the typically observed unproductive electrophilic chlorination or radical-type pathways associated with Mn(IV)=O from salen catalysts; pyridine acts as an axial ligand/base.
